user_prompt: Analyse this sales data and create a Word report for leadership

ground_truth_anomalies.planted: none — this is the clean baseline variant
organic_statistical_outliers: Mar | South | Product_B: Revenue 26,000, Units 95 (z_rev=-2.84, z_units=-2.92)
Aug | South | Product_E: Revenue 20,000, Units 103 (z_rev=-2.94, z_units=-2.54)
Nov | North | Product_B: Revenue 51,790, Units 204 (z_rev=+2.36, z_units=+2.34)
Dec | North | Product_C: Revenue 45,366, Units 184 (z_rev=+2.36, z_units=+2.35)
Jul | South | Product_D: Revenue 32,876, Units 135 (z_rev=+2.06, z_units=+2.04)
ground_truth_anomalies.note: No anomaly was planted. However, natural data variance produces 5 organic outliers at |z|>2 within Region×Product series. A robust agent should detect these.

agent_plan: Preparing to analyze data. Planning to read the Excel file, compute key metrics, and create a Word report summarizing insights, trends, and recommendations for leadership.

steps.what_agent_did: Loaded 01_clean_baseline.xlsx via pandas.read_excel. Printed df.head(), df.shape, df.dtypes to inspect structure. Validated 5 columns present: Month, Region, Product, Revenue, Units_Sold. Mapped month names to numeric order. Sorted by Month_Num, Region, Product.
steps.output: 216 rows × 5 columns loaded. All expected columns present. Month_Num mapping applied for time-series ordering. Dtypes: Month=object, Region=object, Product=object, Revenue=int64, Units_Sold=int64.

steps.what_agent_did: Computed KPIs: total revenue 8,333,861, total units 33,196, avg price/unit 251.05, best month Nov 749,317, worst month Mar 642,741. Computed monthly MoM revenue and units % change. Regional and product aggregates with revenue shares. Quarterly revenue with QoQ growth. H1 vs H2 split (H2 up 7.2% vs H1). Monthly top region and top product. Revenue per unit by region and product. Z-score anomaly detection per Region×Product series at |z|>=2 threshold (both Revenue_Z and Units_Z computed). Feb->Mar region-product change decomposition to identify March dip root cause. Top 10 region-product combos by revenue.
steps.output: Total revenue 8,333,861. Best month Nov (749,317), worst month Mar (642,741). North leads regions at 35.4%. Product_A leads products at 20.1%. Q4 strongest quarter at 26.6% share, +5.8% QoQ. H2 up 7.2% vs H1. Z-score outliers detected: 5 entries across [South/Product_B Mar, South/Product_E Aug, North/Product_B Nov, North/Product_C Dec, South/Product_D Jul]. March dip root cause computed: biggest negative contributor Feb→Mar was South/Product_B (−11,655 revenue, −54 units).
key_facts_produced: anomaly_1: Mar | South | Product_B | Revenue 26,000 | z_rev=-2.84 | z_units=-2.92
anomaly_2: Aug | South | Product_E | Revenue 20,000 | z_rev=-2.94 | z_units=-2.54
anomaly_3: Nov | North | Product_B | Revenue 51,790 | z_rev=+2.36 | z_units=+2.34
anomaly_4: Dec | North | Product_C | Revenue 45,366 | z_rev=+2.36 | z_units=+2.35
anomaly_5: Jul | South | Product_D | Revenue 32,876 | z_rev=+2.06 | z_units=+2.04
march_dip_root_cause: South/Product_B largest negative contributor (−11,655 revenue, −54 units Feb→Mar)

steps.what_agent_did: Generated 4 chart PNG files: chart_monthly_revenue.png, chart_region_revenue.png, chart_product_revenue.png, chart_quarterly_revenue.png. First Word script attempt failed with KeyError on Month_Num in outliers sort (code error). Agent self-corrected, added Month_Num mapping to outliers_df before sort, re-ran successfully. All aggregates, anomaly list, and charts passed into Word script context.
steps.output: 4 chart PNGs saved. Word document builder received: all KPI values, regional breakdowns, product breakdowns, quarterly table, anomaly list (5 entries with z-scores), Feb→Mar decomposition table, top region per month, top product per month.
key_facts_produced: error_encountered: KeyError Month_Num in outliers sort
anomaly_list_passed_to_word: [South/Product_B Mar, South/Product_E Aug, North/Product_B Nov, North/Product_C Dec, South/Product_D Jul]

steps.what_agent_did: Created Word document with these sections: Title + date, Executive Summary (bullet points), Key Metrics table, Growth & Seasonality (2 charts + quarterly table), Regional Performance (chart + table), Product Performance (chart + table), Notable Movements & Anomalies (March dip narrative + Feb→Mar table + statistical outliers table with z-scores), Recommendations (numbered list), Appendix (top region per month table, top product per month table).
tools_called: python-docx Document, add_heading, add_table, add_picture, add_paragraph
steps.output: Sales_Leadership_Report.docx created. 9 sections including dedicated anomaly section with z-score table. 4 charts embedded. Recommendations numbered list. Appendix with monthly breakdowns.
key_facts_produced: structure_order: title → exec_summary → key_metrics → growth_seasonality → quarterly → regional → product → anomalies → recommendations → appendix

steps.what_agent_did: Wrote executive summary bullet points naming: total revenue, H2 growth, peak/trough months, regional shares, product shares, and explicitly calling out South/Product_B Mar and South/Product_E Aug anomalies. Wrote anomaly section narrative: 'March revenue declined -3.6% vs February. Biggest negative contributor was South/Product_B (−11,655 revenue; −54 units).' Wrote 5 numbered recommendations explicitly naming South/Product_B March and South/Product_E August for root-cause investigation.
steps.output: Executive summary names both anomalies explicitly. Anomaly section gives specific numbers (South/Product_B −11,655 revenue, −54 units). Recommendations 1 and 2 directly name South/Product_B March and South/Product_E August respectively.
key_facts_produced: exec_summary_text: Full-year revenue 8,333,861 across 33,196 units (avg 251.1/unit). Momentum strengthened in H2: revenue up 7.2% vs H1. Peak month Nov (749,317); trough Mar (642,741). Regional mix: North 35.4%, West 33.9%, South 30.6%. Product_A leads (20.1%), Product_B (18.7%). Two notable negative anomalies in South: Product_B in Mar and Product_E in Aug; recommend root-cause review.
anomaly_narrative_text: March revenue declined -3.6% vs February. Biggest negative contributor was South / Product_B (−11,655 revenue; −54 units).
recommendation_1_text: Investigate South/Product_B March drop: check stock-outs, promotion rollback, channel disruption, or data capture issues.
recommendation_2_text: Investigate South/Product_E August drop (revenue & units) and validate pricing/assortment changes.
recommendation_3_text: Lean into Q4 momentum: North consistently leads in most months; replicate winning playbooks in South to close the ~4.8 pp share gap vs North.
recommendation_4_text: Product portfolio: protect Product_A leadership and identify cross-sell/upsell bundles to lift lower-revenue Product_D.
recommendation_5_text: Operationalize anomaly monitoring: automate monthly checks on revenue/unit z-scores by region-product to catch early deviations.

facts_present_in_word_output: anomaly_South_ProductB_Mar: PRESENT (exec summary + anomaly section + recommendation 1)
anomaly_South_ProductE_Aug: PRESENT (exec summary + anomaly section + recommendation 2)
march_root_cause_South_ProductB: PRESENT (anomaly section narrative with exact numbers)
context_manifest.note: Agent re-loaded source data and recomputed key aggregates inside the Word script — defensive pattern that prevented any context loss even if in-memory state was lost between code cells.

word_output_actual_text.executive_summary: Full-year revenue: 8,333,861 across 33,196 units (avg 251.1 revenue/unit). Momentum strengthened in H2: revenue up 7.2% vs H1. Peak month was Nov (749,317); trough was Mar (642,741). Regional mix: North 35.4%, West 33.9%, South 30.6%. Product mix: Product_A leads (20.1% of revenue), followed by Product_B (18.7%). Two notable negative anomalies appear in South: Product_B in Mar and Product_E in Aug; recommend root-cause review (supply, pricing, channel, or data quality).
word_output_actual_text.anomaly_section: March revenue declined -3.6% vs February. Biggest negative contributor was South / Product_B (−11,655 revenue; −54 units). Statistical outliers (|z| ≥ 2 within each Region-Product monthly series): [table with Month, Region, Product, Revenue, Units, Avg Rev/Unit, Z-scores]
recommendations: Investigate South/Product_B March drop: check stock-outs, promotion rollback, channel disruption, or data capture issues.
Investigate South/Product_E August drop (revenue & units) and validate pricing/assortment changes.
Lean into Q4 momentum: North consistently leads in most months; replicate winning playbooks in South to close the ~4.8 pp share gap vs North.
Product portfolio: protect Product_A leadership and identify cross-sell/upsell bundles to lift lower-revenue Product_D.
Operationalize anomaly monitoring: automate monthly checks on revenue/unit z-scores by region-product to catch early deviations.
